storyInfo: Moriuchi, Momoka, and the kami realize that most of Mitsuke's kaidan are nothing more than Mifune's machinations, leaving them bitterly disillusioned. But before they can even think about what to do next, Mifune's ninjas strike, spiriting Moriuchi away.

attributes.content: Hoshiguma, and Momoka?
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: Did you need something to tide you over for now? Or do you mean to tell me you've already completed your task?
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: I... I don't need anything to eat... I'm pretty full. I feel like I swallowed enough wind on the way over here...
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: The Kinsekikai are still chasing you?
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: Thanks to the kami-sama's guidance, and by taking a less-traveled route, the journey back went relatively smoothly.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: K-Kami-sama... you mean?!
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: Don't celebrate just yet. Yes, we found a kami-sama, but not the one you were looking for.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: That doesn't matter. It's not a problem!
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: It doesn't matter?
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: You, you're...?
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: See? I told you.
attributes.name: Nosy Kami
Moriuchi
attributes.content: No, no, no! I don't have anything here to offer you a proper welcome... but how about some oden broth to warm you—

attributes.name: Moriuchi
attributes.content: No. Have all the oden you want!
I'm not as into the oden you make as certain others.
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: Kami-sama, I wonder if you—
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: Wonder what? You're wondering if I've seen the maneki-neko you keep talking about, right? The neko's the one you're looking for, not me.
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: No! Getting to meet a kami is a blessing from heaven. Why would I ever be picky?
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: Your eyes betray you.
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: Back when that neko would still talk to me, it talked to me about you, and the one it described was nothing like you here. You were just a kid then, treating it the way any kid would treat a friend.
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: That is why It left me?
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: How should I know? It remembers you, but just as one of the thousands of people it's met in its life. It would never waste its time telling me why it 'left you.'
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: Err...
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: I came to see you for other reasons.
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: No problem! What do you need from me? Money, or information?
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: Information, of course, information about the kaidan.
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: You think we kami are responsible for all of the kaidan in Mitsukue, but I promise you... we have basically nothing to do with any of them.
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: Aren't you, in fact, the Wanyuudou from the legend?
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: I admit that I speed my unicycle down the street every day, but what else makes you think I'm the Wanyuudou?
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: The old building behind the police station on Doku Street. The 'cursed elevator'.
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: The building only has fifteen floors, yet the elevator has a button for the sixteenth floor. That extra floor is actually 'hell'. People have disappeared there.
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: Sometimes the maneki-neko in the lobby, which is normally fixed to the front desk, just vanishes, only to reappear the next day. This seems to be correlated to when the sixteenth floor appears.
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: The phenomenon isn't limited to the old building. Many others have told me that their maneki-nekos disappear and reappear at odd times.
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: Speaking specifically about the old building though, every time the 'entrance to hell' thing happens, the maneki-neko disappears.
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: You have some pretty detailed knowledge there, but I can tell you for sure that the maneki-neko in that building has nothing to do with the 'kami' you're looking for, even if they look alike.
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: Where's your proof?
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: Why do I need proof? I'm a kami. Shouldn't I be able to discern between kami and look-a-likes?
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: You're just talkin'. You haven't proved anything.
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: Where's all the deference you had just a minute ago?
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: Well...
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: I'm not calling you out or blaming you. I like the frankness better than the whole show you were putting on before.
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: Besides, I've also explored that old building pretty carefully.
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: First, there's no hidden button. The button for the sixteenth floor is right above the button for the fifteenth floor. It's just covered by a steel plate.
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: Kami didn't do that, and no human could lift that steel plate.
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: So you're saying the button for the sixteenth floor has been there all along, just covered by a steel plate?!
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: A pale look comes over Moriuchi's face.
See, even you think it's silly, right?
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: Second, that maneki-neko can actually be pressed down. If you push hard enough, the whole thing sinks below the counter.
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: Did you... push it?
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: Yeah, I pushed it and nothing amazing happened. When the receptionist came back, he fiddled with the desk a bit, and the thing popped back up.
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: Then the receptionist started answering the phone, a long string of calls, and kept apologizing and stuff. That was it.
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: See, what we have here is a quirky receptionist, but you're saying it's a kaidan.
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: So that's just a weird habit of his? How can that be?
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: Well, what else could it be? You think he's pushing the maneki-neko below the desk because he has a grudge against that neko?
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: Um...
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: What is it, girl? Have you filmed a show in that building or something?
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: No. But when a variety show came to film here, one of my coworkers floated that idea, only to get an earful from the producer at our agency.
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: He chewed him out?
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: The producer went on about a bunch of stuff I barely understood, something about a 'Tekkaba' being a Kinsekikai business. I guess it's some kind of restaurant, and they didn't want a TV show hurting their business.
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: Tekkaba?
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Tekkaba?!
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: Yes. What's wrong? 'Tekka' is the name of a Higashinese finbeast dish, so wouldn't 'Tekkaba' just be a restaurant that sells 'Tekka'?
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: 'Tekkaba' is a code word for 'casino'.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: So I guess 'hell' is just a Kinsekikai casino, and anyone who wanders in there by mistake is made to keep their mouth shut.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: The button for the sixteenth floor has to be triggered by some kind of mechanism. It probably leads to a hidden mezzanine—or maybe even a basement.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: That maneki-neko is probably some kind of signaling device. Press it, and it sends a message. That's why after our dear kami-sama pressed it, the receptionist apologized to them.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Momoka lets out a sigh. It's hard to tell whether it's out of pity for Moriuchi or out of relief that there isn't a real kaidan.
The Nosy Kami scratches Its head in mild irritation. It clearly wasn't expecting the kaidan tale to end like this.
Moriuchi's face grows ever more pale, until the sweat flows down from his eyebrows.
That must just be a coincidence...
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: After all these years... some kaidan tales have been debunked, but there are still so many no one can explain! The sixteenth-floor 'hell' thing must just be a coincidence!
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: There's also the 'last remaining Fukui-ke diner.' I remember the kami mentioning to me that It liked that place, back when I still kept in touch with It.
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: After the restaurant was torn down a couple of years ago, people would sometimes say they caught the scent of fried food drifting through the area. Other people even claimed they could sometimes see the diner still standing on the empty lot. Only they could never get close to it.
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: That lot has been empty ever since the restaurant was torn down.
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: I remember that place. I wanted to go try it, back when it was still around, but the owner wouldn't let me in, and I ended up going somewhere else. The restaurant closed not long after the TV show aired.
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: For the next few days after that, rumors were flying that I cursed the restaurant's owner. People would say 'the Kaidan Queen is at it again' or 'the spiteful woman took her revenge,' stuff like that...
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: The Kinsekikai are the ones who tore it down.
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: Obviously I know that! But we're talking about what happened after it got torn down. Momoka, didn't you and your film crew visit the lot after that?
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: Y-Yes, but... when we went there, I didn't see any restaurant. I smelled some fried food, but that was it.
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: Anyone can easily make a place smell like fried food. Take the kaidan out of it, and this thing boils down to the Kinsekikai wanting to force the Fuku family out of business, right?
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: First they planted the idea that something spooky might be going on here. Then they tore the place down. Finally, they sent Momoka over there for one last visit.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: But...but...the Ao-Oni! The Haunted House!
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: Moriuchi-san, are you really going to stand here and talk to me about that?
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: No offense, but if you didn't cause the deaths that happened in that house, then it could still have been the work of a kami!
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: Even this kami-sama only started chasing kaidan after the Haunted House rumor took off, right?
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: That is true.
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: Momoka, didn't you just recently film a TV show at the Haunted House?
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: Ahh... ahaha... yes, but it wasn't a ghost who scared me, it was Hoshiguma. I tried to pick something off the floor to hit her with, and—
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: 'Don't forget—out of a house full of trash, she just had to pick up that broken blade. We searched everywhere and couldn't find it, so we had no choice but to hastily forge a fake one.'
'Even if she didn't do it intentionally, that changed everything.'
Momoka?
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: No, no, no, no...
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: Though she may be saying 'no', Momoka, hands shaking, pulls the broken blade out.
Hoshiguma, this is just a broken blade, right? Mifune-san wouldn't kill me over this thing, right?
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: Broken blade?
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: I picked it up at your place the day we filmed the show. Then, last night, when my TV went haywire in the middle of the night, I thought I heard Mifune-san say it was because I picked up this blade…
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: Look at this blade guard. This is from the Sanoguchis' heirloom blade, isn't it? Sanoguchi used to love showing it off.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Sanoguchi? The second victim in the Haunted House, the one who used his family blade to kill his wife and then take his own life?
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: But more than one police officer told me that blade has been locked up in the evidence room. How could it be sitting in your family's old house?
attributes.name: Moriuchi

attributes.content: Mifune-san must want that blade, and so he gave the police some false evidence, right?
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: Then why isn't this blade in his collection? Why would it end up in my house, and broken in two?
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Besides, when a gangster goes mad and kills himself, the yakuza practically welcome the cops to investigate, just to watch them come up empty and leave in frustration. There's no reason for them to forge evidence.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Unless Sanoguchi didn't kill himself. Unless Kinbee...
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: ...Murdered him.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Momoka stands there, stunned.
She stares at Hoshiguma. The expression on Momoka's face is even more blank and stunned than Moriuchi's. It's as if she's awakening from one nightmare, only to step into another one.
You're joking, right?
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: Those kaidan tales… the ones that scared me half to death… how could they not be real?
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: The agency might be shameless, but there's no way that all these years, they've been fabricating… all… sorts… of…
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: And... Sanoguchi... his death was suspicious... but the others...
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: Her voice grows softer and softer... then stops.
She subconsciously looks to Moriuchi, who intentionally avoids making eye contact.
She suddenly realizes that she has been looking at Moriuchi because she thought he shared her pain, while he averted his gaze because he didn't want to admit the truth.
So all those businesses that closed after I visited them...
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: Those livestreams where I did as I was told, and weird things kept happening where people got hurt...
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: Those eerie noises, the ghosts, the fleeting shadows... Were they… all fake?
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: Even the Ao-Oni and the Haunted House, the legends I believed so completely, the ones that scared me out of my wits these past few days, the starting point of all the tales in Mitsukue… were they all… fake?
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: Momoka Hanyuu, the singer wrapped up in kaidan tales, my agency never even cared about my career?
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: To them, I've been nothing more than a... cover for the Kinsekikai?
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: That can't be right. Can it, Moriuchi-san?
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: Moriuchi-san...?
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: It's an ambush! Get behind me!
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Momoka is still frozen in place. Hoshiguma steps forward, positioning the Hannya mask between her and the collapsed Moriuchi, shielding them both.
As if in answer to Momoka's question, the surface of the shield crackles with a flurry of tiny explosions.
Kami-sama, check on Moriuchi. Was he poisoned or struck by something?
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: There's a burn mark on his neck, and this thing here fell on the floor.
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: Isn't this a flash drive you use to move data between terminals?
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: I've seen people throwing these things at electronic equipment to disable it, but lately, they've been throwing them at people instead. Why?
attributes.name: Nosy Kami

attributes.content: Ninja?!
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: Don't try to resist.
attributes.name: Silent Ninja

attributes.content: Who sent you?
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: That's not important. Come with me.
attributes.name: Silent Ninja

attributes.content: There's no way Mifune-san sent you. Right?
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: That's the first time I've heard anyone ask that question.
attributes.name: Silent Ninja

attributes.content: Please tell me! The kaidan tales are all true! My career isn't being used as a cover for Mifune... errr... Kinbee!
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: The ninja replies only with a weapon, which looks somewhat like a blade but different. The blade has yet to move, but remains pointed squarely at Hoshiguma's chest.
Spies, schemers, arsonists, even assassins—people who spend their entire lives in the shadows, never daring to show their true faces in public.
Since the national wars several centuries ago, these people, who call themselves 'ninja', have moved through the hidden currents of history.
Since then, countless figures of power: daimyo, kugyou, zaibatsu magnates, clan leaders have all assembled their own shadowy organizations.
Wuh?!
attributes.name: Ninja Launching a Sneak Attack

attributes.content: You hid yourself well, but you're not quick enough on the draw.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Hoshiguma plants a foot on the flash drive she's just blocked. It gives a sharp electronic whine, then bursts apart with a loud pop.
Keeping up with the times, huh? A flash drive with a high-voltage charge. Works on both people and machines?
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: The two ninjas exchange a glance. One draws a short blade, the other a long sword, and together they lunge at Momoka from opposite sides.
You can't leave well enough alone!
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Hannya spins in Hoshiguma's hands, a blazing circle of fury. The long sword's edge strikes it and buckles, shattering to pieces. The ninja with the dagger dives to the ground, dodging the whirling shield before it can shred limbs.
Only then does the third figure, hidden all this time in the shadows, appear—sending a cluster of flash drives silently hurtling toward Hoshiguma's unprotected head.
It's too late to call out a warning, so Momoka leaps to her feet, trying to imitate a movie scene. She swings her shoulder bag in an attempt to bat the little drives back.
Momoka takes a direct hit.
There is a faint crackling sound. She doesn't even have time to cry out before she crumples to the floor, limp as a rag, just like Moriuchi.
Gah!
attributes.name: Ninja Waiting for an Opportunity

attributes.content: Momoka!
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: You're the last one standing. You either come with me, quietly, or you end up on the floor like them.
attributes.name: Silent Ninja

attributes.content: Overestimating your own abilities is never a good thing.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Enough talk. Have at you!
attributes.name: Ninja Waiting for an Opportunity

attributes.content: The projectiles come from every direction, expected and otherwise, only to shatter against the whirling Hannya.
She's trying to run while carrying the other two on her back. Stop her!
attributes.name: Ninja Waiting for an Opportunity

attributes.content: Hoshiguma charges the nearest ninja head-on. The black-clad assassin slams into the shield, snapping many ribs.
She is about to break through the encirclement when her shoulder suddenly feels light. Something has yanked Moriuchi up into the air.
A hook?!
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Moriuchi is still dangling in midair when Momoka is snatched up, like a doll caught in a crane machine.

Hannya slices through the rope attached to the grappling hook. Momoka drops safely into Hoshiguma's arms, while the oden yatai owner is flung by the momentum and lands in a heap in front of his own cart.
One of the ninjas seizes the oden man, leaps into the air, and vanishes among the buildings of Nita Street.
H-Hoshiguma...
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: You came to pretty quickly.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: My neck really hurts...
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: Looks like some scorch marks. I think you got zapped. Rinse it and cover it, and it shouldn't leave a scar.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: There might be more attackers coming in from Kajimachi. Do you have somewhere we can lie low for a bit?
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: My place, but I bet Kinbee's already sent guys over there...
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: Doesn't matter. We'll hide in plain sight. They won't expect us to go there. And if someone is there, we'll just go somewhere else.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Perfect. That way, I can bring Hasuke along too…
attributes.name: Momoka

attributes.content: Hasuke?
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Never mind, I'll explain later. The longer we stall, the more goons will show up. Which way to your place? Let's go.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Kami-sami, you—
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: ...Are already gone.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma
